sutta: monks there are these 8 bases of indolence 1 8 here in mounts among has to do some work and he thinks there's some work for me to do but the doing of it will tire me physically well i'll lie down and he lies down without putting forth energy to attain the unattained to master the unmastered to realize the unrealized this is the first basis of indolence or he has done some work and things that he is tired and lies down without putting forth energy this is the second basis of indolence or he has to make a journey and thinks that it will tire him and he lies down without putting forth energy this is the third basis or he has made a journey and things that he's tired when he lies down without putting forth energy this is the fourth basis all wandering for arms through village or town and not getting enough course or dainty fare for his needs he thinks i've wandered for arms through village and town and have not gotten enough cause or dainty fare for my needs my body is tired and unpliable well i lie down and he does so without putting forth energy this is the fifth basis or getting enough and he thinks that his body is heavy and unpliable like a load of soap beans and he lies down without putting forth energy this is the 6 basis or there arises some slight illness in the monk and he thinks this slight illness has arisen there is good reason to lie down well i'll lie down and he lies down without putting forth energy this is the seventh basis again monks a mom has recovered from some ailment has arisen recently from sickness and things i've recovered recovered from that ailment i am recently arisen from sickness and my body is weak and unpliable well i lie down and he lies down without putting forth energy to attain the unattained to master the unmastered to realize the unrealized this is the eighth basis of indolence verily monks these are the 8 bases of indolence amongst these are the 8 basis of energy 1 8. here in monks there is some work to be done by among many things there's some work for me to do but if i do it not easy will it be to fix my mind on the buddha's message well i provide for that and put forth energy to attain the unattained to master the unmastered to realize the unrealized and he puts forth energy to attain the unattained to master the unmastered to realize the unrealized this month is the first basis of energy or he has a journey and thinks that it will not be easy to fix his mind on the buddha's message so should put forth energy to attain the unattained and he does so this is a third basis or he has made a journey and thinks that he has been unable to fix his mind on the buddha's message so should put forth energy to attain the unattained and he does so this is the fourth basis all wandering from arm for arms through village or town and not getting his needs he thinks that his body is buoyant and pliable and then he should put forth energy to attain the unattained and he does so this is the fifth basis or getting enough food he thinks that his body is strong and pliable and that he should put forth energy to attain the unattained and he does so this is the 6 pieces or there arises some slight illness in him and he thinks that it is possible that his illness may grow worse and that he should put forth energy to attain the unattained and he does so this is the seventh basis again monks a man has recovered from some ailment has arisen recently from sickness and he thinks i've recovered from that ailment i'm a reason recently arisen from sickness it is possible that that ailment may return to unmastered to realize the unrealized this monks is the eighth basis of energy early amongst these are the 8 bases of energy that's the end of the sutra from this suta you can see the buddha is advising his disciples to be very energetic in their practice even though they are tired and then they should practice instead of lying down even though they have just recovered from a sickness they should practice instead of lying down etc and that shows the character of the buddha the buddha himself had such a strong willpower that he did all these things and that is why he became a samasan
